skill.name: fastapi-templates
skill.description: Create production-ready FastAPI projects with async patterns, dependency injection, and comprehensive error handling. Use when building new FastAPI applications or setting up backend API projects.
skill.summary: Create production-ready FastAPI projects with async patterns, dependency injection, and comprehensiv...
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution findings are false positives caused by Markdown code fences and inline code. The real risk is that the skill presents production-ready FastAPI templates with permissive CORS and SQL echo logging, which users could copy into deployed applications.

medium_findings.title: Permissive CORS Defaults in Production Template
medium_findings.description: The FastAPI example is described as production-ready but configures CORS with wildcard origins, all methods, all headers, and credentials enabled. This can expose authenticated browser requests if copied into a deployed service without restriction.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact template lines show wildcard CORS settings together with credential support. This is a well-known insecure default for production APIs.

medium_findings.title: Verbose SQL Logging in Application and Test Templates
medium_findings.description: The database engine examples set SQLAlchemy echo logging to true. In deployed or shared environments, this can disclose query structure and sensitive parameter values through logs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code examples explicitly enable echo logging. It is a moderate risk because this is template guidance, not executable marketplace code.

low_findings.title: Static Command-Execution Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The Ruby or shell backtick detections point to Markdown code fence delimiters and inline code formatting, not executable shell commands. No evidence found of command invocation behavior in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review confirms these are Markdown delimiters for examples. The file contains no executable script entry point or shell command call.

low_findings.title: Environment and Secret References Are Template Configuration
low_findings.description: The DATABASE_URL, SECRET_KEY, and .env references are part of a FastAPI settings example. They do not read marketplace host secrets, but they require users to manage application secrets securely.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The references appear only inside illustrative Python snippets. There is no evidence of secret collection, logging, or network transmission by the skill.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Is a Local Test Client Base URL
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL detection points to an httpx AsyncClient base URL for tests. It uses the reserved test host and does not contact an external service in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is contained in a test fixture example and uses http://test. No evidence found of data exfiltration or outbound network behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Insecure Production Defaults in Example Code
dangerous_patterns.description: The template combines production-oriented wording with CORS and SQL logging defaults that should be restricted before deployment.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The risky defaults are directly visible in the template. The impact depends on whether users copy them unchanged into deployed applications.

content.value_statement: Starting a FastAPI backend often requires many repeated architecture decisions. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured templates for async APIs, services, repositories, auth, and tests.
actual_capabilities: Suggests a layered FastAPI project layout with API, core, models, schemas, services, and repositories.
Provides async SQLAlchemy session and repository patterns for CRUD-style services.
Shows service-layer examples for user creation, password hashing, authentication, and updates.
Includes route examples that use FastAPI dependencies, response models, and HTTP error handling.
Provides pytest and httpx examples for async API integration tests.
limitations: It is guidance-only and does not automatically create project files.
Referenced assets and detailed reference files are not included in this package.
Security-sensitive defaults such as CORS origins and SQL logging need review before production use.
Examples require adaptation for the selected database, auth model, deployment target, and FastAPI version.

use_cases.title: Start a New API Backend
use_cases.description: Create an initial FastAPI structure with routes, configuration, database access, services, and tests.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team API Architecture
use_cases.description: Use consistent repository and service patterns across multiple Python web services.

use_cases.title: Add Auth and Testing Patterns
use_cases.description: Extend an early FastAPI project with token authentication examples and async integration tests.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic FastAPI Layout
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this FastAPI templates skill to propose a simple project layout for a new async REST API. Include folders, responsibilities, and the first routes to implement.

prompt_templates.title: Design CRUD Layers
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design repository, service, schema, and route layers for a FastAPI resource named [resource]. Explain how requests should flow through each layer.

prompt_templates.title: Add Auth and Database Sessions
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to add authentication, settings, and async database session patterns to my FastAPI project. Call out any values that must come from secure environment configuration.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Production API Scaffold
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my FastAPI scaffold for production readiness. Focus on dependency injection, async database usage, auth boundaries, CORS configuration, logging, and test coverage.

output_examples.input: Create a FastAPI structure for a user and items API.
output_examples.output: A layered project plan with API routers, Pydantic schemas, SQLAlchemy models, repositories, services, settings, and test folders.

output_examples.input: Add authentication to an async FastAPI project.
output_examples.output: A design for token creation, password hashing, current-user dependencies, protected routes, and settings that load secrets from environment configuration.

output_examples.input: Review my FastAPI scaffold before deployment.
output_examples.output: A checklist of production concerns, including restricted CORS origins, disabled SQL echo logging, secure secrets, database session handling, and async test coverage.

best_practices: Ask for project structure first, then request code for one layer at a time.
Replace wildcard CORS origins with explicit trusted origins before deployment.
Keep secrets, database URLs, and token settings in managed environment configuration.
anti_patterns: Do not copy the example CORS settings directly into production.
Do not leave SQL echo logging enabled for deployed services.
Do not mix route handlers, database queries, and business rules in one file.

faq.question: Does this skill generate a complete FastAPI project automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides templates and guidance that an AI assistant can use to create or review project files.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it include database examples?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes async SQLAlchemy session, repository, and test database patterns.

faq.question: Does it include authentication guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes password hashing, token creation, and current-user dependency examples.

faq.question: Is the template ready for production without changes?
faq.answer: No. Review CORS, logging, secrets, database settings, and deployment configuration before production use.

faq.question: Are external reference files included?
faq.answer: No evidence found in the file structure. This package contains SKILL.md only.
